laws.title: Definition of Consumer
laws.meaning: Consumer means any person who buys goods or avails services for personal use and not for commercial resale.

laws.title: Complaint before District Commission
laws.meaning: A consumer complaint can be filed before the District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission for claims up to Rs. 1 crore.

laws.title: Jurisdiction of State Commission
laws.meaning: State Consumer Commission handles complaints where value of goods/services and compensation claimed exceeds Rs. 1 crore but does not exceed Rs. 10 crore.

laws.title: Punishment for False/Misleading Advertisement
laws.meaning: Manufacturer or endorser of a false or misleading advertisement may be punished.
laws.punishment: Up to 2 years imprisonment and Rs. 10 lakh fine for first offence

laws.title: Compensation for Failure to Protect Data
laws.meaning: A body corporate holding sensitive personal data is liable to pay compensation if it fails to protect such data.
laws.punishment: Compensation as determined by Adjudicating Officer

questions.text: What type of consumer complaint is this?
What is the approximate value of the complaint (in Rs.)?
When was the product purchased / service availed?
Did you make a complaint to the company / service provider?
Has the company responded to your complaint?
Do you have the purchase receipt/invoice?

recommended_actions: Send written complaint / legal notice to company before filing consumer case
File complaint at District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission
Lodge complaint on National Consumer Helpline (1800-11-4000)
File complaint on Consumos online portal at consumos.gov.in
Contact RERA authority for real estate builder disputes
Approach Banking Ombudsman for banking service complaints
File complaint with Insurance Ombudsman for insurance disputes
Approach TRAI for telecom related complaints
